Bloom's Taxonomy Applied to AWS Learning

Design architectures
Build security solutions
🔴 EVALUATE
Review architectures
Justify tradeoffs
🟢 ANALYZE
Troubleshoot issues
Compare services

Hands-on labs
Build projects

Explain concepts
Summarize services

Recall facts
Identify services
Cognitive Level Key — Each week is tagged to a Bloom's level
Remember
Understand
Apply
Analyze
Evaluate
Create
Phase 1 — Cloud Practitioner
Phase 2 — SAA-C03
Phase 3 — SAP-C02
Phase 4 — Security Specialty
01

AWS Cloud Practitioner (CLF-C02)

Build your foundational mental model of cloud computing. Understand AWS's global infrastructure, core service categories, pricing, and the shared responsibility model. Heavy focus on Remember and Understand.

🎯 Target: AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner
Weeks 1–6
WK 01
Cloud Concepts & AWS Global Infrastructure
Remember
▼

📖 Theory (2 hrs/day)

  • What is cloud computing? IaaS / PaaS / SaaS
  • AWS Regions, Availability Zones, Edge Locations
  • AWS Global infrastructure map
  • Benefits of cloud: elasticity, agility, cost
  • AWS Well-Architected Framework intro

🧪 Labs (1.5 hrs/day)

  • Create your AWS Free Tier account
  • Explore AWS Console — all service categories
  • Set up IAM user, MFA, billing alerts
  • Use AWS CLI basic commands

📝 Flashcards / Recall

  • Anki deck: AWS service names & categories
  • Quiz: Cloud deployment models
  • Daily: 20 practice questions on cloud basics
WK 02
Core Services — EC2, S3, RDS, VPC
Understand
▼

📖 Theory

  • EC2 instance types, AMIs, pricing models
  • S3 storage classes & lifecycle policies
  • RDS vs DynamoDB — when to use each
  • VPC basics: subnets, IGW, route tables

🧪 Labs

  • Launch EC2, SSH in, host a web page
  • Create S3 bucket, enable versioning
  • Set up a simple VPC with public subnet
  • Explore RDS — create a MySQL instance

💻 GitHub Project #1

  • Repo: aws-fundamentals-labs
  • Document all lab steps in README
  • Add architecture diagrams (draw.io)
WK 03
Security, IAM & Shared Responsibility
Understand
▼

📖 Theory

  • AWS Shared Responsibility Model (deep dive)
  • IAM: users, groups, roles, policies
  • Security groups vs NACLs
  • AWS Shield, WAF, Inspector, GuardDuty intro

🧪 Labs

  • Create IAM policies from scratch (JSON)
  • Set up CloudTrail for auditing
  • Enable GuardDuty in free tier

📝 Cert Prep

  • 50 CLF-C02 practice questions (focus: security)
  • Review AWS pricing calculator
  • AWS Support plans comparison
WK 04–05
Billing, Pricing, Support & Exam Prep
Apply
▼

📖 Theory

  • AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)
  • Cost Explorer, Budgets, Trusted Advisor
  • AWS Organizations & consolidated billing
  • Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF)

📝 Exam Prep

  • Whizlabs / Udemy CLF-C02 practice exams
  • Take 3 full mock exams (65 questions each)
  • Review every wrong answer in depth
  • Target score: 800+ before booking

💻 Interview Prep

  • Explain Shared Responsibility to a non-technical person
  • Q: "What's the difference between S3 and EBS?"
  • Q: "When would you use Spot vs Reserved instances?"
WK 06
✅ Take CLF-C02 Exam + Reflect
Evaluate
▼

🎯 Exam Week

  • Final review of weak areas only
  • Book and take CLF-C02 exam
  • Rest 1 day before exam

📊 After Exam

  • Post score to GitHub README (accountability)
  • Identify gaps to fill before SAA
  • Write a reflection post (dev.to / LinkedIn)
02

AWS Solutions Architect Associate (SAA-C03)

Deepen your ability to Apply and Analyze AWS services to design resilient, scalable, and cost-optimized architectures. Build real projects and document them on GitHub.

🎯 Target: AWS SAA-C03
Weeks 7–14
WK 07–08
Compute, Auto Scaling & Load Balancing
Apply
▼

📖 Theory

  • EC2 deep dive: placement groups, ENI, EBS types
  • Auto Scaling Groups: policies, health checks
  • ELB: ALB vs NLB vs CLB — use cases
  • Lambda fundamentals & invocation models
  • ECS vs EKS vs Fargate overview

🧪 Labs

  • Build an ASG with ALB from scratch
  • Configure ALB path-based routing
  • Deploy a Lambda function with API Gateway
  • Stress test ASG to trigger scale-out

💻 GitHub Project #2

  • Repo: aws-ha-web-architecture
  • 3-tier app: ALB → ASG EC2 → RDS Multi-AZ
  • CloudFormation or Terraform IaC included
  • Architecture diagram required
WK 09–10
Storage, Databases & Networking Deep Dive
Analyze
▼

📖 Theory

  • S3 advanced: replication, Object Lock, Glacier
  • EFS, FSx, Storage Gateway use cases
  • DynamoDB: partitions, GSI, streams
  • ElastiCache: Redis vs Memcached
  • VPC: peering, Transit Gateway, Direct Connect
  • Route 53: routing policies (failover, latency, geo)

🧪 Labs

  • Build S3 cross-region replication pipeline
  • DynamoDB: CRUD + GSI query optimization
  • VPC peering between two VPCs
  • Route 53 failover routing with health checks

💻 GitHub Project #3

  • Repo: serverless-data-pipeline
  • S3 → Lambda → DynamoDB pipeline
  • SNS notifications on pipeline events
  • All resources via CloudFormation
WK 11–12
IAM Advanced, Security & Well-Architected
Analyze
▼

📖 Theory

  • IAM roles for services, cross-account access
  • SCP in AWS Organizations
  • KMS, CloudHSM, ACM — encryption deep dive
  • Well-Architected 6 pillars applied to designs
  • Disaster Recovery strategies: RPO/RTO

🧪 Labs

  • Cross-account role assumption
  • KMS encrypt/decrypt S3 objects
  • Run Well-Architected Review on your project
  • CloudWatch alarms + SNS alerts

📝 Cert Prep

  • 100+ SAA-C03 practice questions
  • Study the official SAA exam guide
  • Focus on scenario-based questions
WK 13–14
Mock Exams, Projects Polish & ✅ SAA Exam
Evaluate
▼

📝 Exam Prep

  • 5 full mock exams (65 questions each)
  • Target: 85%+ before booking
  • Review all AWS whitepapers: Security, HA, Cost
  • Interview Q practice: architecture trade-offs

💻 Portfolio Polish

  • Finalize all 3 GitHub repos with good READMEs
  • Add architecture diagrams to each project
  • Write a blog post on your SAA journey
03

AWS Solutions Architect Professional (SAP-C02)

Evaluate and Create complex multi-account architectures. This is the hardest AWS cert — expect deep dives into migration, networking, cost optimization, and org-level design.

🎯 Target: AWS SAP-C02
Weeks 15–20
WK 15–16
Multi-Account Strategy & Organizational Design
Evaluate
▼

📖 Theory

  • AWS Organizations: OU hierarchy design
  • AWS Control Tower — landing zone setup
  • Service Control Policies: design & guardrails
  • AWS RAM (Resource Access Manager)
  • Multi-account networking: Transit Gateway hub-spoke

🧪 Labs

  • Design a 3-OU org structure (sandbox/prod/security)
  • Implement SCP deny policies
  • Cross-account resource sharing with RAM
  • Centralized logging account architecture

💻 GitHub Project #4

  • Repo: aws-multi-account-landing-zone
  • Terraform: full org + SCP + logging account
  • Document design decisions
WK 17–18
Migrations, Hybrid Cloud & Advanced Networking
Evaluate
▼

📖 Theory

  • 7Rs of migration (re-host, re-platform, etc.)
  • AWS Migration Hub, DMS, SMS, Snowball
  • Direct Connect + VPN failover patterns
  • Hybrid DNS with Route 53 Resolver
  • Data transfer cost optimization

🧪 Labs

  • Simulate on-prem → AWS DB migration with DMS
  • Configure Site-to-Site VPN
  • Route 53 Resolver inbound/outbound rules

📝 SAP Exam Prep

  • Jon Bonso SAP-C02 practice exams (hardest available)
  • Read AWS Migration whitepaper
  • Practice 75-question timed exams
WK 19–20
Cost Optimization, Final Mock Exams & ✅ SAP Exam
Create
▼

📖 Theory

  • Cost optimization patterns across all services
  • Savings Plans vs Reserved vs Spot strategies
  • Architectural anti-patterns — what NOT to do
  • Catastrophic failure patterns & mitigation

💻 Capstone Design

  • Design a full enterprise AWS architecture from scratch
  • Present trade-offs for each decision
  • Cost estimate with AWS Pricing Calculator
  • Repo: aws-enterprise-architecture-capstone
04

AWS Certified Security Specialty (SCS-C02)

The ultimate goal. Deep-dive into threat detection, identity federation, data protection, incident response, and compliance. Pure Create and Evaluate cognitive work.

🎯 Target: AWS Certified Security Specialty
Weeks 21–24
WK 21
Identity, Federation & Secrets Management
Analyze
▼

📖 Theory

  • IAM deep dive: permission boundaries, session policies
  • AWS SSO / IAM Identity Center
  • SAML 2.0 & OIDC federation patterns
  • Cognito: user pools vs identity pools
  • Secrets Manager vs Parameter Store

🧪 Labs

  • SAML federation with IAM roles
  • Cognito-protected API Gateway endpoint
  • Secrets Manager rotation with Lambda
  • Implement permission boundary guardrails

💻 GitHub Project #5

  • Repo: aws-zero-trust-identity
  • Zero-trust architecture with Cognito + API GW
  • Least-privilege IAM with permission boundaries
WK 22
Threat Detection, Monitoring & Incident Response
Evaluate
▼

📖 Theory

  • GuardDuty: finding types, suppression rules
  • Security Hub: standards, findings aggregation
  • AWS Config: rules, conformance packs
  • CloudTrail: management vs data events
  • Macie: S3 data classification
  • Incident response playbooks on AWS

🧪 Labs

  • Simulate GuardDuty findings (tester tool)
  • Build automated IR with EventBridge + Lambda
  • Config rule: enforce S3 block public access
  • Security Hub aggregation across regions

💻 GitHub Project #6

  • Repo: aws-automated-incident-response
  • GuardDuty → EventBridge → Lambda → SNS auto-remediation
  • Full runbook documentation
WK 23
Data Protection, Encryption & Network Security
Create
▼

📖 Theory

  • KMS deep dive: key policies, grants, cross-account
  • CloudHSM: when to use over KMS
  • S3 encryption: SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, SSE-C, DSSE
  • AWS WAF: rules, managed rule groups, rate limits
  • Network Firewall vs WAF vs Shield Advanced
  • VPC endpoints: Gateway vs Interface

🧪 Labs

  • KMS key rotation, cross-account access
  • WAF rules blocking SQL injection / XSS
  • VPC endpoint policy for S3
  • Network Firewall: stateful rule groups

💻 GitHub Project #7

  • Repo: aws-secure-data-platform
  • End-to-end encryption: upload → store → access
  • WAF + Shield + CloudFront protection layer
WK 24
Final Projects, Mock Exams & ✅ Security Specialty Exam
Create
▼

💻 Final Capstone Project

  • Repo: aws-security-reference-architecture
  • Multi-account security baseline (Control Tower + Security Hub + GuardDuty + Config)
  • Automated compliance checking pipeline
  • Full threat model documentation
  • This is your portfolio centrepiece 🏆

📝 Final Prep

  • 5 full SCS-C02 mock exams
  • Target: 85%+ before booking
  • Review all security whitepapers

🎙️ Interview Prep

  • "How would you detect a compromised IAM key?"
  • "Design a HIPAA-compliant AWS architecture"
  • "What's your IR process for a GuardDuty finding?"
  • "Explain least-privilege to an exec"
🏆 Certification Journey
CLF-C02
Cloud Practitioner
Target: Week 6 · Entry Level
SAA-C03
Solutions Architect Associate
Target: Week 14 · Associate
SAP-C02
Solutions Architect Professional
Target: Week 20 · Professional
SCS-C02
Security Specialty
Target: Week 24 · Specialty 🎯

📚 Top Courses

  • Stephane Maarek — SAA, SAP, Security (Udemy)
  • Adrian Cantrill — SAA, SAP (cantrill.io)
  • A Cloud Guru / Pluralsight
  • AWS Skill Builder (official, free tier)
  • TutorialsDojo — practice exams

🧪 Hands-On Labs

  • AWS Free Tier — always-on sandbox
  • AWS CloudQuest (gamified labs)
  • Whizlabs hands-on labs
  • LocalStack — free local AWS emulator
  • AWS Well-Architected Labs (free)

💻 GitHub Strategy

  • 1 repo per major topic/project
  • Every repo: README + architecture diagram
  • Use IaC (Terraform or CloudFormation)
  • Tag repos with bloom-level achieved
  • Pin 6 best repos to your profile

📄 Key Whitepapers

  • AWS Security Best Practices
  • AWS Well-Architected Framework
  • AWS Cloud Adoption Framework
  • Architecting for the Cloud
  • AWS Disaster Recovery Whitepaper

AWS Certification Exam Guide

All domains, tasks, services, and scenario questions drawn directly from the official AWS Certification Exam Guides. Coverage: CLF-C02 · SAA-C03 · SAP-C02 · SCS-C03

☁
CLF-C02

AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner

Entry-level certification validating overall AWS Cloud knowledge. Tests understanding of cloud concepts, AWS core services, security, and billing independent of a specific job role.

65
Questions
700
Pass Score
90m
Duration
3yr
Valid
Domain 1
24%
Cloud Concepts
  • Define the benefits of the AWS Cloud (global reach, agility, elasticity, pay-as-you-go)
  • Identify design principles of the AWS Well-Architected Framework (6 pillars)
  • Understand benefits and strategies for migration to AWS (7Rs, CAF)
  • Understand cloud economics (CapEx vs OpEx, TCO, economies of scale, BYOL)
Domain 2
30%
Security and Compliance
  • Understand the AWS Shared Responsibility Model
  • Understand cloud security, governance, and compliance concepts
  • Identify AWS access management capabilities (IAM, root user, MFA, least privilege)
  • Identify components for security (WAF, Shield, GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie)
Domain 3
34%
Cloud Technology and Services
  • Define methods of deploying and operating in AWS (Console, CLI, SDK, IaC)
  • Define AWS global infrastructure (Regions, AZs, Edge Locations)
  • Identify compute services (EC2, Lambda, ECS, EKS, Fargate, Auto Scaling)
  • Identify database services (RDS, Aurora, DynamoDB, ElastiCache, DMS)
  • Identify network services (VPC, Route 53, CloudFront, VPN, Direct Connect)
  • Identify storage services (S3, EBS, EFS, FSx, Storage Gateway, Backup)
  • Identify AI/ML and analytics services (SageMaker, Bedrock, Rekognition, Kinesis)
Domain 4
12%
Billing, Pricing, and Support
  • Compare AWS pricing models (On-Demand, Reserved, Spot, Savings Plans)
  • Understand resources for billing, budget, and cost management (Cost Explorer, Budgets)
  • Identify AWS support plan types (Basic, Developer, Business, Enterprise)
  • Understand AWS Organizations for consolidated billing and SCPs
Key Services Tested

📝 CLF-C02 Practice Questions

🏗
SAA-C03

AWS Solutions Architect – Associate

Validates ability to design secure, resilient, high-performing, and cost-optimized AWS solutions based on the Well-Architected Framework. 1 year hands-on experience recommended.

65
Questions
720
Pass Score
130m
Duration
3yr
Valid
Domain 1 — 30%
Design Secure Architectures
IAM · Encryption · Network Security
  • Task 1.1: Design secure access to AWS resources (IAM, STS, SCPs, cross-account)
  • Task 1.2: Design secure workloads and applications (VPC, SG, NACL, Shield, WAF, Secrets Manager)
  • Task 1.3: Determine appropriate data security controls (KMS, ACM, encryption at rest/transit, S3 Object Lock)
Domain 2 — 26%
Design Resilient Architectures
HA · Fault Tolerance · Loose Coupling
  • Task 2.1: Design scalable and loosely coupled architectures (SQS, SNS, Event-driven, microservices, serverless)
  • Task 2.2: Design highly available and fault-tolerant architectures (Multi-AZ, Multi-Region, DR strategies: RPO/RTO, backup/restore, pilot light, warm standby, active-active)
Domain 3 — 24%
Design High-Performing Architectures
Performance · Scalability · Caching
  • Task 3.1: High-performing storage (S3, EBS gp3/io2, EFS, FSx, hybrid)
  • Task 3.2: Elastic compute solutions (EC2 families, Lambda, Fargate, ECS/EKS, Auto Scaling)
  • Task 3.3: High-performing databases (Aurora, DynamoDB+DAX, ElastiCache, RDS replicas, purpose-built DBs)
  • Task 3.4: Scalable network architectures (CloudFront, Global Accelerator, NLB/ALB, VPN, Direct Connect)
  • Task 3.5: Data ingestion & transformation (Kinesis, Glue, Athena, EMR, DataSync, Lake Formation)
Domain 4 — 20%
Design Cost-Optimized Architectures
Cost · Rightsizing · Pricing Models
  • Task 4.1: Cost-optimized storage (S3 lifecycle, Intelligent-Tiering, EBS type selection, storage tiering)
  • Task 4.2: Cost-optimized compute (Spot, Reserved, Savings Plans, serverless vs EC2, Compute Optimizer)
  • Task 4.3: Cost-optimized databases (RDS vs Aurora pricing, DynamoDB on-demand vs provisioned)
  • Task 4.4: Cost-optimized networks (VPC Endpoints, CloudFront origin cost, data transfer minimization)
15 Critical SAA-C03 Heuristics
01
High Availability = Multiple AZs ALWAYS. A single-AZ deployment is never the correct answer for HA.
02
Decoupled architecture = SQS between tiers. Prevents cascade failures and enables independent scaling.
03
Globally distributed = CloudFront + Route 53. Edge locations reduce latency and protect origins.
04
No operational overhead = Managed/Serverless always wins. Lambda > Fargate > EC2 for event-driven.
05
Shared storage across EC2 = EFS (POSIX NFS). EBS is AZ-locked and cannot be shared across instances.
06
Sub-millisecond DB reads = DynamoDB + DAX. ElastiCache Redis for any other in-memory caching needs.
07
Secrets in application code = Never hardcode. Always Secrets Manager (rotation) or Parameter Store SecureString (static).
08
Analytics on S3 data = Athena (serverless SQL, pay per query). Glue for ETL. Redshift for ongoing BI workloads.
09
IoT / real-time streaming = Kinesis Data Streams (ingest) → Kinesis Data Firehose (deliver) → S3/Redshift.
10
Migrate on-prem DB = DMS (homogeneous: same engine) + SCT (heterogeneous: different engine, schema conversion).
11
Private on-prem connection = Direct Connect (dedicated, consistent bandwidth). VPN is encrypted but over public internet.
12
DR strategies in cost order: Backup & Restore (cheapest, slowest) → Pilot Light → Warm Standby → Active-Active (costliest, fastest).
13
Fan-out messaging = SNS (topic) → multiple SQS queues (subscribers). Never SNS direct to Lambda at high volume.
14
Cost for S3 egress = S3 Gateway VPC Endpoint eliminates NAT Gateway cost ($0.045/GB) for S3/DynamoDB traffic.
15
RDS connection exhaustion = Deploy RDS Proxy. Pools connections. Mandatory for Lambda → RDS patterns (Lambda scales to thousands).
In-Scope AWS Services

📝 SAA-C03 Practice Questions

⚡
SAP-C02

AWS Solutions Architect – Professional

Advanced exam validating deep technical skills in designing optimized enterprise AWS solutions. Builds on SAA. Tests organizational complexity, multi-account strategy, migration, and modernization.

75
Questions
750
Pass Score
180m
Duration
3yr
Valid
Domain 1 — 26%
Design for Organizational Complexity
Multi-Account · Networks · Resilience
  • Task 1.1: Architect network connectivity strategies (VPC peering, TGW, Direct Connect, PrivateLink, hybrid DNS)
  • Task 1.2: Prescribe security controls (IAM Identity Center, KMS, CloudTrail, Security Hub, Inspector, cross-account access)
  • Task 1.3: Design reliable and resilient architectures (RTO/RPO, Elastic DR, pilot light, warm standby, multi-site)
  • Task 1.4: Design multi-account AWS environments (Organizations, Control Tower, OUs, SCPs, RAM)
  • Task 1.5: Cost optimization and visibility (Trusted Advisor, Cost Explorer, Compute Optimizer, rightsizing, tagging)
Domain 2 — 29%
Design for New Solutions
Deployment · Continuity · Performance
  • Task 2.1: Design a deployment strategy (IaC/CloudFormation, CI/CD, blue/green, canary, Systems Manager)
  • Task 2.2: Ensure business continuity (Route 53 routing policies, Multi-AZ, Multi-Region, automated DR testing)
  • Task 2.3: Determine security controls (least privilege IAM, DDoS mitigation, encryption at rest/transit, patch management)
  • Task 2.4: Meet reliability requirements (loosely coupled, SNS/SQS/Step Functions, Auto Scaling, DNS routing)
  • Task 2.5: Meet performance objectives (right-sizing, caching, buffering, purpose-built databases, elastic architecture)
  • Task 2.6: Cost optimization strategy (Reserved vs Spot vs Savings Plans, data transfer costs, VPC Endpoints)
Domain 3 — 25%
Continuous Improvement for Existing Solutions
Ops Excellence · Security · Performance
  • Task 3.1: Improve operational excellence (CloudWatch alerting, automated remediation, CI/CD pipelines, Config rules)
  • Task 3.2: Improve security (secrets management, least privilege audit, traceability, vulnerability remediation, patching)
  • Task 3.3: Improve performance (auto scaling, Global Accelerator, CloudFront, monitoring, bottleneck identification)
  • Task 3.4: Improve reliability (replication, scaling, high availability, eliminate single points of failure)
  • Task 3.5: Cost optimizations (rightsizing, Spot Instances, tagging, Cost and Usage Reports, billing alarms)
Domain 4 — 20%
Accelerate Workload Migration and Modernization
7Rs · Migration · Modernization
  • Task 4.1: Select workloads for migration (Migration Hub, portfolio assessment, wave planning, TCO evaluation)
  • Task 4.2: Optimal migration approach (DataSync, Snow Family, DMS, SCT, Application Migration Service, Direct Connect)
  • Task 4.3: New architecture for existing workloads (EC2, Beanstalk, ECS, EKS, Aurora, DynamoDB, OpenSearch)
  • Task 4.4: Modernization opportunities (Lambda, Fargate, S3, EFS, purpose-built DBs, EventBridge, Step Functions)
SAP-C02 Anti-Patterns — Never Choose These
✗
Single-AZ for stateful services in production. Always Multi-AZ for RDS, ElastiCache, EFS.
✗
Static credentials in code. Always IAM roles + Secrets Manager. Rotate with Lambda function.
✗
NAT Gateway for S3/DynamoDB traffic. Use VPC Gateway Endpoints — they're free and eliminate NAT costs.
✗
Root account for daily operations. Break-glass only. Enable MFA, no access keys on root.
✗
One monolithic VPC for everything. Separate VPCs per env, connected via Transit Gateway.
✗
Lambda for long-running tasks >15min. Use ECS Fargate + Step Functions for orchestration.
✗
DynamoDB Scan operations in production. Always design access patterns and use Query with GSIs.
✗
Publicly accessible RDS. Always private subnet + Security Groups. Never public endpoint.
In-Scope AWS Services

📝 SAP-C02 Practice Questions

🛡
SCS-C03

AWS Certified Security – Specialty

Intended for individuals responsible for securing cloud solutions. Validates knowledge of AWS data protection, incident response, infrastructure security, IAM, and compliance. 3–5 years experience recommended.

65
Questions
750
Pass Score
170m
Duration
3yr
Valid
Domain 1 — 16%
Detection
Monitoring · Logging · Alerting
  • Design monitoring and alerting solutions (GuardDuty, Security Hub, Macie, Security Lake, CloudWatch dashboards)
  • Design logging solutions (CloudTrail organization trail, CloudWatch Logs agent, VPC Flow Logs, Route 53 Resolver logs)
  • Analyze logs (CloudWatch Logs Insights, Athena, OpenSearch, Lambda normalization, Managed Grafana)
  • Troubleshoot security monitoring and alerting solutions (Lambda permission checks, CloudTrail delivery failures)
Domain 2 — 14%
Incident Response
Plans · Response · Forensics
  • Design and test incident response plans (Systems Manager OpsCenter, SageMaker AI notebooks, Shield Advanced)
  • Use AWS Fault Injection Service and Resilience Hub for IR testing and validation
  • Automate remediation (Step Functions, Lambda, Application Recovery Controller, Automated Forensics Orchestrator)
  • Capture forensic artifacts, correlate logs, contain and eradicate threats, restore from backups
Domain 3 — 18%
Infrastructure Security
Edge · Compute · Network Controls
  • Edge security: CloudFront headers, WAF rules (OWASP Top 10), Shield Advanced, geolocation/rate limiting
  • Compute security: hardened AMIs, container image scanning (ECR), Systems Manager Patch Manager, EC2 Image Builder
  • Network controls: Security Groups, NACLs, Network Firewall, Firewall Manager, PrivateLink, VPC Flow Logs
  • VPN and encryption in transit: Site-to-Site VPN, Direct Connect MACsec, TLS with ACM
Domain 4 — 20%
Identity and Access Management
IAM · Federation · Least Privilege
  • IAM identity management at scale (IAM Identity Center, Directory Service, SAML 2.0 federation, ABAC)
  • AWS account access and authorization (permission boundaries, SCPs, resource policies, cross-account roles)
  • Audit and validate IAM configurations (Access Analyzer, Credential Report, Access Advisor, CloudTrail)
  • Manage privileged access (AWS Organizations, Control Tower, AWS Config rules for IAM compliance)
Domain 5 — 18%
Data Protection
Encryption · Keys · Secrets
  • Data classification (Macie for S3 sensitive data discovery, tagging, lifecycle policies)
  • Plan and implement data encryption at rest (KMS CMKs, SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, CloudHSM for FIPS 140-2 Level 3)
  • Plan and implement data encryption in transit (ACM TLS, private CA, certificate rotation)
  • Secrets management (Secrets Manager rotation, Parameter Store SecureString, cross-account secret sharing)
Domain 6 — 14%
Security Foundations and Governance
Compliance · Governance · Frameworks
  • Implement and manage security policies (AWS Config conformance packs, Security Hub standards: CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA)
  • AWS Audit Manager for continuous compliance evidence collection
  • Artifact for compliance reports (SOC 1/2/3, PCI, ISO) and agreements (BAA, NDA)
  • Well-Architected Security Pillar review, Detective for threat investigation, Firewall Manager for organization-wide WAF
Critical Security Services Deep Reference
KMS
Key Management Service: Customer-managed keys (CMKs), automatic rotation (annual), envelope encryption, multi-region keys, CloudHSM integration. Used by: S3, EBS, RDS, DynamoDB, Lambda, SQS.
GD
GuardDuty: ML-based threat detection. Analyzes: CloudTrail events, VPC Flow Logs, DNS logs, S3 data events. Detects: crypto-mining, credential compromise, C2 traffic. 50GB free trial.
IAM
Permission Boundaries: Set maximum permissions an identity can have — even if their policies allow more. Used to safely delegate admin tasks. Cannot grant permissions beyond the boundary.
SCPs
Service Control Policies: Organization-level guardrails. Restrict what accounts CAN do — they don't grant permissions. Account must have BOTH SCP AND IAM policy permissions. Deny overrides allow.
CF
CloudHSM: Single-tenant FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSMs. Customer-managed keys — AWS has NO access. Required for: PKI CA, TLS offloading, Oracle TDE encryption. More expensive than KMS.
WAF
WAF + Firewall Manager: WAF rules at CloudFront/ALB/API GW/AppSync. Firewall Manager: centrally manage WAF rules across all accounts in Organization. Shield Advanced: 24/7 DRT, $3,000/month/org.
In-Scope AWS Services

📝 SCS-C03 Practice Questions

⚙
DOP-C02

AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional

Validates advanced skills in provisioning, operating, and managing distributed application systems. Covers CI/CD, IaC, monitoring, incident response, and security automation.

75
Questions
750
Pass Score
180m
Duration
3yr
Valid
Domain 1 — 22%
SDLC Automation
  • Design and implement CI/CD pipelines (CodePipeline, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy, CodeCommit)
  • Integrate automated testing (unit, integration, load testing, canary deployments)
  • Deployment strategies: blue/green, canary, rolling, all-at-once with automated rollback
  • Container and serverless deployment (ECR, ECS, EKS, Lambda, SAM)
Domain 2 — 17%
Configuration Management and IaC
  • CloudFormation: nested stacks, StackSets, drift detection, change sets, custom resources
  • AWS CDK for programmatic infrastructure definition
  • Systems Manager: State Manager, Patch Manager, Parameter Store, Run Command, Session Manager
  • Service Catalog for governance of approved infrastructure templates
Domain 3 — 15%
Resilient Cloud Solutions
  • Multi-AZ and multi-Region architectures, Auto Scaling policies, health checks
  • Self-healing infrastructure with EC2 Auto Recovery, ASG lifecycle hooks
  • Chaos engineering with AWS Fault Injection Service
Domain 4 — 15%
Monitoring and Logging
  • CloudWatch: custom metrics, Logs Insights, Container Insights, alarms, dashboards, Contributor Insights
  • X-Ray for distributed tracing and service maps
  • Managed Grafana and Prometheus for metrics visualization
  • Centralized logging: CloudWatch → Kinesis Data Firehose → S3 → Athena
Domain 5 — 14%
Incident and Event Response
  • EventBridge rules for automated response to AWS events
  • Systems Manager OpsCenter for incident tracking, runbooks with Automation documents
  • AWS Fault Injection Service for controlled chaos experiments
Domain 6 — 17%
Security and Compliance
  • AWS Config rules and conformance packs for continuous compliance
  • Secrets Manager rotation, CodePipeline secrets scanning, Inspector for vulnerability management
  • SecurityHub aggregating findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, Config

📝 DOP-C02 Practice Questions

🤖
MLS-C01

AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty

Validates ML/DL knowledge and ability to design, implement, deploy, and maintain ML solutions using AWS services. Covers data engineering, modeling, deployment, and MLOps.

65
Questions
750
Pass Score
180m
Duration
3yr
Valid
Domain 1 — 20%
Data Engineering
  • Create data repositories (S3 Data Lake, Lake Formation, Redshift, RDS, DynamoDB, Timestream)
  • Identify and implement data ingestion solutions (Kinesis Data Streams/Firehose, DMS, DataSync, Snow Family)
  • Identify and implement data transformation (Glue ETL, EMR Spark, Lambda, Athena, Managed Flink)
  • Data formats: CSV → Parquet (columnar, 70% smaller), JSON, ORC, Avro for ML pipelines
Domain 2 — 24%
Exploratory Data Analysis
  • Sanitize and prepare data (handle missing values: imputation vs deletion, outlier detection)
  • Feature engineering (normalization, standardization, one-hot encoding, TF-IDF)
  • Data analysis and visualization (SageMaker Studio, QuickSight, Athena SQL queries)
  • Handle imbalanced datasets (SMOTE, class weights, stratified sampling, undersampling)
Domain 3 — 36%
Modeling
  • Frame ML problems: choose algorithm (XGBoost, Linear Learner, Random Forest, Neural Networks)
  • Train ML models: compute choice (GPU vs CPU, distributed training, Spot Instances)
  • Hyperparameter optimization: regularization (L1/L2/dropout), cross-validation, SageMaker HPO
  • Evaluate models: AUC-ROC, F1, RMSE, confusion matrix, A/B testing, offline vs online evaluation
  • Avoid overfitting: validation sets, early stopping, dropout, data augmentation
Domain 4 — 20%
ML Implementation and Operations
  • Build performant, available, scalable ML solutions (SageMaker endpoints, Auto Scaling, Multi-AZ)
  • Recommend appropriate ML services (SageMaker built-in vs custom, Bedrock for GenAI, Rekognition/Polly/Transcribe)
  • Apply security to ML: IAM roles, S3 bucket policies, VPC endpoints, SageMaker VPC mode
  • Deploy and operationalize (real-time endpoints, batch transform, SageMaker Pipelines for MLOps, Model Monitor)
